query: The attached spreadsheet shows the inventory for a movie and video game rental store in Seattle, Washington. What is the title of the oldest Blu-Ray recorded in this spreadsheet? Return it as appearing in the spreadsheet.

agents.name: WebSurfer
agents.system_prompt: A helpful assistant with access to a web browser. Performs web searches, opens pages, and interacts with content (clicking links, scrolling, filling forms). Can summarize pages or answer questions based on page content.

agents.name: FileSurfer
agents.system_prompt: An agent that can handle local files, read their contents, and extract information.

agents.name: Coder
agents.system_prompt: A helpful and general-purpose AI assistant with strong language skills, Python skills, and Linux command line skills. Writes code for the Executor agent to run.

agents.name: Executor
agents.system_prompt: An agent that executes code blocks (Python, shell) and returns the output.

agents.name: MagenticOneOrchestrator
agents.system_prompt: The orchestrator that coordinates the team. Creates plans, delegates tasks to agents, and synthesizes results.

Here is an initial fact sheet to consider:

1. GIVEN OR VERIFIED FACTS  
- The file in question is located at: /NHNHOME/WORKSPACE/0426030030_A/jungwoonshin/tips_project/dataset/gaia/files/2023/validation/32102e3e-d12a-4209-9163-7b3a104efe5d.xlsx  
- The file is an Excel spreadsheet.  
- The spreadsheet contains inventory for a movie and video game rental store in Seattle, Washington.  
- The format of interest is Blu-Ray.  
- The goal is to find the title of the oldest Blu-Ray as recorded in the spreadsheet.  
- The spreadsheet contains multiple sections: DVDs, Blu-Rays, and Video Games.  
- The relevant data rows include columns: Title, Genre, Year, Platform, Status.  
- The Blu-Ray section begins after the DVD section and is labeled "Blu-Ray".  
- The "Year" column represents the release year of the title.  

2. FACTS TO LOOK UP  
- Confirm the exact column headers and their positions in the spreadsheet.  
- Identify which rows fall under the "Blu-Ray" section based on the label in the first column.  
- Extract all titles in the "Blu-Ray" section along with their corresponding "Year".  
- Verify if there are any variations in format labeling (e.g., "Blu-ray", "BD") — though in this case, the section is explicitly labeled "Blu-Ray".  

3. FACTS TO DERIVE  
- Determine which Blu-Ray entry has the earliest (minimum) year.  
- Among titles released in the same earliest year, select the one that appears in the data (no tie-breaking needed unless duplicates exist).  
- Return the title exactly as it appears in the "Title" column for that earliest Blu-Ray.  

4. EDUCATED GUESSES  
- The "Year" column likely refers to the release year of the movie or game.  
- The oldest Blu-Ray is likely from the earliest year listed under the "Blu-Ray" section.  
- Based on the structure, the first few rows under the "Blu-Ray" header are the relevant ones for this query.  
- The title "Time-Parking 2: Parallel Universe" appears twice (once under DVD and once under Blu-Ray), both with the year 2009, suggesting it may be the oldest Blu-Ray if no earlier years appear in that section.  
- No Blu-Ray in the list appears to have a year earlier than 2009 within the Blu-Ray section.  
- Therefore, the oldest Blu-Ray is likely "Time-Parking 2: Parallel Universe" with a release year of 2009.  



Here is the plan to follow as best as possible:

- Task FileSurfer to access and read the Excel file located at `/NHNHOME/WORKSPACE/0426030030_A/jungwoonshin/tips_project/dataset/gaia/files/2023/validation/32102e3e-d12a-4209-9163-7b3a104efe5d.xlsx`.

- Extract all rows under the "Blu-Ray" section where the "Title" and "Year" columns are populated.  
- Filter these entries to find the one with the minimum (earliest) "Year".  
- Return the "Title" of that entry exactly as it appears in the spreadsheet.  
- If needed, task Coder to write a Python script to parse the Excel file, filter Blu-Ray titles, and determine the oldest by year.  
- Task Executor to run the Python script if automation is required for accuracy and efficiency.  
- Avoid using WebSurfer as no external lookup is necessary — all data is contained in the local file.
